patch.value: The 100 percent docstring gates (source/exported/member) incentivized mass template docstrings that satisfy coverage while carrying zero information: grep counts 622 'Implements X for the public runtime surface of this module', 2,945 'Value that configures or reports X for this contract' across 135 files, plus 543 'Provides CLI runtime support for X'/'payload exchanged by command, SDK, and package integrations' variants - about 4,100 filler lines. Cost: inflated file length (the 3400-LOC gate deliberately excludes comment lines, but agents reading files still pay the tokens), noise for agent context assembly, and docs that answer nothing (example: ExtensionCommandArgumentHelpDescriptor.name documented as 'Value that configures or reports name for this contract'). This is content-quality simplification under pm-n7rr; distinct from pm-fb3i (coverage floors) and pm-gvnj (DeepSource docs category).
Replace template filler docstrings (about 4,100 occurrences) with informative docs and lint against the filler phrases

Census script or lint rule flags the known filler templates (Implements X for the public runtime surface of this module; Value that configures or reports X for this contract; Provides CLI runtime support for X; payload exchanged by command, SDK, and package integrations) and the count is ratcheted down, not up; Touched-file burn-down policy documented so gate-mandated docstrings added going forward must carry real information (what the value means, units, invariants) or use @inheritDoc/type-level docs; Static-gate docstring coverage stays at 100 percent throughout - the fix is content quality, never coverage relaxation
